preamble: A rare but documented shift where a rhotic [r] triggers or evolves into a retroflex nasal [ɳ], often in the context of nasalization spread or specific morphophonological environments in Indo-Aryan development.

examples.note: Indirect triggers in Nati; rare direct shifts in some Middle Indo-Aryan developments.

sources: Masica, C. P. (1991). The Indo-Aryan Languages.
Fortson, B. W. (2010). Indo-European Language and Culture: An Introduction.
